---
name: Feature request
about: Propose a change to the Sovereign Portal framework or a reference module
title: "[feature] "
labels: ["enhancement", "triage"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
Before opening:

Sovereign Portal is intentionally minimal. The framework provides identity, multi-tenancy, audit, the 4-layer permission model, the module registry, and four reference modules (shell, users, companies, workspaces). Everything else belongs in your own modules.

Read CONTRIBUTING.md → "What belongs in this repo" before opening. Most feature requests we receive belong in a downstream module, not in the framework.

If you want a custom feature built into your deployment, that is a paid engagement — see SUPPORT.md.
-->

## The problem

<!-- What are you trying to do that you cannot do today? Describe the use case, not the proposed solution. -->

## Why this belongs in the framework (not in your own module)

<!--
This is the most important section. The Sovereign Portal grading question is:
"Does this make the reference clearer, more correct, or more portable?"

If your feature only helps your specific deployment, it belongs in your own module.
If it helps every Sovereign Portal deployment, explain why here.
-->

## Proposed approach

<!-- Optional. If you have an implementation in mind, sketch it. Keep it short — the framework prefers small, conservative additions. -->

## Alternatives considered

<!-- What else did you consider? Why is this the right shape? -->

## Scope check

- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md → What belongs in this repo](../../CONTRIBUTING.md#what-belongs-in-this-repo)
- [ ] This change makes the reference clearer, more correct, or more portable — not just better for my specific deployment
- [ ] I am willing to submit a pull request if the proposal is accepted
- [ ] I understand that breaking changes to public APIs require a deprecation cycle

---

<sub>Need a custom module or framework extension built for your deployment? That is a [Path 3 engagement](../../SUPPORT.md#path-3--have-us-build-it) — Freshify builds sovereign modules to spec.</sub>
